bestInnings: Brendon McCullum's 158* launched the league with a statement innings.
bestBowling: Sohail Tanvir's 6/14 became the season's defining bowling spell.
moments: Yusuf Pathan delivers in the final
summary: The first IPL season felt like a new sport had arrived. Rajasthan turned a modest squad into the league's first champion through fearless roles and sharp leadership.

bestInnings: Adam Gilchrist's semi-final burst flipped the knockout mood.
bestBowling: Anil Kumble's 4/16 nearly dragged RCB to the trophy.
summary: A relocated IPL produced one of the league's best redemption arcs. Deccan Chargers transformed from 2008 strugglers into champions.

bestInnings: Suresh Raina's unbeaten 57 shaped the final.
bestBowling: Doug Bollinger's new-ball pressure helped CSK control the chase.
moments: Dhoni's core becomes a dynasty seed
summary: Chennai's first title established the template: batting depth, calm captaincy, and knockout clarity.

bestInnings: Murali Vijay's 95 turned the final into a yellow wave.
bestBowling: Malinga's yorker-heavy season set the pace standard.
summary: CSK became the IPL's first repeat champion, winning at home with authority.

bestInnings: Manvinder Bisla's 89 stunned CSK in Chennai.
bestBowling: Sunil Narine's mystery spin controlled the season's middle overs.
summary: Kolkata's first title arrived through spin control, Gambhir's leadership, and one unforgettable final chase.

bestInnings: Pollard's unbeaten 60 rescued Mumbai in the final.
bestBowling: Bravo's 32-wicket season set a high bar for death-over all-rounders.
summary: Mumbai's first championship turned a talented squad into a trophy-winning machine.

bestInnings: Manish Pandey's 94 gave KKR a title chase at full speed.
bestBowling: Mohit Sharma's cutters and discipline made him the season's leading wicket-taker.
summary: A high-scoring final captured the league's attacking shift, with KKR chasing 200 to win the trophy.

bestInnings: Rohit Sharma's 50 in the final set Mumbai's tone.
bestBowling: Bravo's second Purple Cap season underlined his T20 control.
summary: Mumbai recovered from a slow start and peaked at the right time, a pattern that became part of their identity.

bestInnings: Kohli's 973-run season remains the IPL batting Everest.
bestBowling: Bhuvneshwar Kumar led a title-winning attack with swing and death control.
summary: RCB owned the spectacle, but SRH owned the final through bowling balance and Warner's leadership.

bestInnings: Krunal Pandya's 47 became priceless in a low-scoring final.
bestBowling: Mumbai's collective final squeeze defended 129 by a single run.
summary: The 2017 final was pure tension: Mumbai defended almost nothing and won their third title by one run.

bestInnings: Shane Watson's unbeaten 117 sealed CSK's comeback title.
bestBowling: Andrew Tye's variations made him the season's leading wicket-taker.
summary: CSK's comeback season became a celebration of experience, trust, and one of the great final hundreds.

bestInnings: Andre Russell's all-season power made him the tournament's force.
bestBowling: Bumrah's final spell was elite pressure bowling.
summary: Mumbai and Chennai produced another razor-edge final, with Bumrah and Malinga closing the door.

bestInnings: KL Rahul's 132* was the season's biggest individual batting peak.
bestBowling: Rabada's 30 wickets carried Delhi's pace threat.
moments: The IPL moves to the UAE
summary: In a closed-door UAE season, Mumbai looked deeper and more complete than everyone else.

bestInnings: Faf du Plessis' 86 in the final gave CSK control.
bestBowling: Harshal Patel's 32 wickets tied the single-season record.
summary: CSK turned a poor previous season into another title, powered by top-order stability and veteran clarity.

bestInnings: Buttler's four-hundred season powered Rajasthan's run.
bestBowling: Chahal's control and wickets made him the Purple Cap winner.
moments: Gujarat win in their debut season
summary: Gujarat Titans entered the IPL and won immediately, with Hardik Pandya delivering leadership and final impact.

finalScore: CSK 171/5 beat GT 214/4 by 5 wickets via DLS
bestInnings: Gill's late-season hundreds made him the tournament's batting star.
bestBowling: Shami's new-ball wickets made Gujarat's powerplay dangerous.
moments: Gill produces a historic run season
summary: A rain-stretched final ended with Jadeja's last-ball finish and CSK level with Mumbai on five titles.

bestInnings: Kohli's second Orange Cap season blended volume with improved tempo.
bestBowling: Starc's knockout spells peaked at the perfect time.
summary: Kolkata built the season's most complete side, then crushed the final with pace, spin, and fearless batting.

bestInnings: Sai Sudharsan's run tally made him the breakout batting leader.
bestBowling: Prasidh Krishna's wicket-taking season delivered the Purple Cap.
moments: Krunal Pandya shapes the title match
summary: RCB's long wait ended in Ahmedabad, turning years of fan pressure into the franchise's first IPL trophy.

bestInnings: Virat Kohli's unbeaten 75 in the final completed RCB's chase with 12 balls left.
bestBowling: Kagiso Rabada finished the season on top of the wicket chart with 29 wickets.
moments: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi wins MVP and Orange Cap
Kagiso Rabada seals the Purple Cap
summary: RCB became back-to-back IPL champions, beating Gujarat Titans in Ahmedabad while Sooryavanshi and Rabada topped the season charts.
